mission.title: Install and Configure Dalec on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dalec is a project aimed at providing a declarative format for building system packages and containers with a focus on supply chain security. Installing Dalec allows you to produce secure packages and containers efficiently using simple configurations.

steps.title: Add the Dalec Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Dalec Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add dalec https://project-dalec.github.io/dalec/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dalec using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dalec in the 'dalec' namespace (it will be created if it doesn't exist) with the following command:
```bash
helm install dalec dalec/dalec --namespace dalec --create-namespace --version v0.20.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Dalec pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dalec
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the Dalec deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment dalec --namespace dalec
``` 
Then add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the Dalec service, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/dalec --namespace dalec 8080:80
``` 
You can now access Dalec at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dalec will show the pods running when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace dalec`. You can also access the Dalec service through port forwarding, confirming that the installation was successful.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add dalec https://project-dalec.github.io/dalec/charts
helm repo update
helm install dalec dalec/dalec --namespace dalec --create-namespace --version v0.20.3

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and have installed Helm and kubectl.
